findings.evidence: `rg` shows the new theorem at `PathSpace/Shift.lean:65` and the existing general helper at `Exchangeability/Contractability.lean:133`: `(pathLaw μ X).map (fun x => prefixProj α n (fun k => x (φ k))) = blockLaw μ X (fun i => φ i.val)`; taking `φ := fun k => k + r` gives the new block law after rewriting `((shift α)^[r] x)` by `shift_iterate_apply`.
findings.fix: Make the generic `map_reindex_prefixProj_pathLaw` available in the appropriate path-space/basic file and derive this shift case from it, or omit the shift-specific theorem if callers can use the generic lemma plus `shift_iterate_apply`.
findings.issue: `map_prefixProj_shift_iterate_pathLaw` is the `φ k = k + r` special case of the existing generic prefix-reindex path-law lemma, creating a parallel shift-only API for finite blocks.

summary: The PR still adds a shift-specific finite-block path-law theorem that overlaps the existing generic reindexing helper rather than exposing/reusing the general form.
